text.title: Type Guards and Discriminated Unions
text.summary: Type predicates, discriminated unions, and assertion functions for safe narrowing of unknown or union types.

rule.statement: Write type predicates (value is T) instead of casting to narrow unknown values.
rule.rationale: Type predicates make the narrowing condition explicit and reusable; casting bypasses the compiler check entirely.

section.title: Discriminated Unions

rule.statement: Model success/failure responses as a discriminated union with a boolean discriminant, not as a nullable return value.
rule.rationale: Discriminated unions force the caller to handle both branches; nullable returns are easy to ignore and carry no error information.

section.reference: Discriminated union Result type + assertDefined helper

rule.statement: Use assertion functions (asserts value is T) to throw on null/undefined and narrow the type for subsequent code.
rule.rationale: Assertion functions remove repeated null checks while retaining narrowing benefits, unlike the non-null assertion operator (!) which is invisible at runtime.

antipattern.description: Using the non-null assertion operator (!) on values that could legitimately be null.
antipattern.reason: ! silently produces a runtime error; assertDefined throws with a message and narrows the type correctly.
